mission: Preview Generation Code Analysis - Compare preview_189542-98.png to admin canvas rendering
analysis_type: Code Flow Comparison - Preview PNG Generation vs Admin Canvas Reconstruction

preview_generation_code.key_finding: Preview images (preview_189542-98.png) are generated CLIENT-SIDE using Fabric.js canvas.toDataURL(), NOT server-side PHP

preview_generation_flow: 1. User designs on frontend Fabric.js canvas
3. Fabric.js canvas.toDataURL() exports canvas as PNG data URL
4. Data URL sent to server and saved as preview_189542-98.png
5. Preview image reflects EXACT canvas state at export time
admin_rendering_flow: 1. Admin retrieves design_data JSON from WooCommerce order meta
4. Rendered preview displayed in admin canvas
critical_discovery.fundamental_difference: Preview PNG = EXACT canvas screenshot. Admin render = COORDINATE RECONSTRUCTION from JSON.

preview_png_generation.element_positioning: Fabric.js internal coordinate system with automatic centering/transforms
preview_png_generation.transformations: All Fabric.js transformations BAKED INTO PNG (scaleX, scaleY, angle, originX, originY)
preview_png_generation.zoom_handling: Canvas zoom level affects final PNG output

admin_canvas_reconstruction.code_reference: admin/js/admin-canvas-renderer.js - renderImage() method uses EXACT JSON coordinates
admin_canvas_reconstruction.critical_finding: Admin renderer uses RAW COORDINATES without applying Fabric.js originX/originY centering

differences_found: Preview PNG has Fabric.js automatic element centering (originX: 'center', originY: 'center')
Admin render uses raw left/top coordinates WITHOUT originX/originY adjustments
Preview PNG captures visual appearance with all transforms baked in
Admin render reconstructs from mathematical coordinates only

fabric_js_default_behavior.description: Fabric.js defaults to centering elements at their (left, top) coordinates

admin_renderer_behavior.description: AdminCanvasRenderer treats (left, top) as top-left corner, NOT center point

centering_logic_analysis.discrepancy_explanation: Fabric.js canvas.toDataURL() captures visual output where elements are centered. Admin renderer reconstructs using left/top as corner coordinates, causing offset. The 'centered' appearance in preview_189542-98.png is the CORRECT Fabric.js rendering. Admin canvas shows offset because it doesn't apply originX/originY transforms.

save_logic_investigation.how_preview_is_saved: Client-side: Fabric.js canvas.toDataURL('image/png') → Data URL → AJAX upload to server → Saved as preview_PRODUCTID-VARIATIONID.png
additional_transformations: Fabric.js applies all object transforms (scaleX, scaleY, angle)
Fabric.js respects originX='center', originY='center' during rendering
Canvas zoom level (setZoom()) affects final output scale
All visual effects (shadows, filters, opacity) baked into PNG
coordinate_adjustments: transformCoordinates() applies mockup offset: x + offsetX, y + offsetY

code_snippets.note: Uses img.left and img.top directly without centering adjustment

hypothesis: Preview shows centered layout because Fabric.js canvas.toDataURL() captures the VISUAL RENDERING with originX='center'/originY='center' applied. Admin canvas shows offset layout because AdminCanvasRenderer treats left/top as TOP-LEFT CORNER coordinates instead of CENTER POINT coordinates. The discrepancy is caused by missing Fabric.js origin transform logic in the admin renderer.

root_cause.issue: Admin canvas renderer missing Fabric.js originX/originY transform logic
root_cause.impact: Elements appear offset in admin canvas compared to saved preview PNG
root_cause.evidence: Console logs show transform magnitude of 0.00px in admin canvas, confirming NO centering transform applied
root_cause.fix_required: Implement originX/originY coordinate adjustment in AdminCanvasRenderer.renderImage() method

recommended_fix.change: Adjust element positioning to account for Fabric.js originX/originY defaults
recommended_fix.code_example:
if (originX === 'center') { effectiveLeft -= (width * scaleX) / 2; }
if (originY === 'center') { effectiveTop -= (height * scaleY) / 2; }
recommended_fix.rationale: Match Fabric.js rendering behavior to achieve 1:1 replica of preview PNG

validation_steps: 1. Check if design_data JSON contains originX/originY properties
2. Verify current AdminCanvasRenderer ignores these properties
